# AI Awareness & Your Child's Learning (Part 1)

**Event ID:** E1062  
**Source:** https://wisdomhomeschooling.com/events/workshops/workshops/computers-programming/2862-ai-awareness-your-child-s-learning-part-1-b-e1062

## Details

| | |
|---|---|
| **Date** | Monday, March 16, 2026 |
| **Time** | 7:00–8:30 PM |
| **Format** | Live online + recording for registrants |
| **Cost** | $25 |
| **Registration Closes** | Friday, March 13, 2026 |
| **Contact** | Kyra Tenant |

## Description

We begin with what matters most: your child's learning and development. We'll consider how AI tools may influence attention, creativity, and study habits, and what responsible use can look like as children grow into teens and prepare for university. Then we'll demystify the current AI landscape—what today's tools can and can't do and where they're heading—including text-based systems (like chatbots), image creation, and video creation/editing.

## Takeaways

- A clear view of how AI relates to children's development and learning
- Age-aware guidance for families and older teens approaching university
- A high-level understanding of where AI is today and what's coming next

## Included

- A concise handout with explanations, key definitions, and curated resources
- A short pre-event poll (sent a few weeks in advance) so we can tailor the session
- Recording link if you can't attend live

## Notes

This is Part 1 of a two-part series. Part 2 focuses on practical, day-to-day use of AI in homeschooling. We recommend attending this session first.

## Instructor

Gerson Neil Nazareth — 25+ years in software, serves as a CTO. Uses AI extensively in professional software development and is committed to helping families use AI responsibly.

# AI Awareness Part 1 — Preparation Project

**Event Date:** March 16, 2026 (7:00–8:30 PM)  
**Contact:** Kyra Tenant

---

## Tasks

### [ ] Get attendee list from Kyra
**When:** ~March 9, 2026 (1 week before)  
**Notes:** Kyra sends list of signups about a week before the workshop

### [ ] Send welcome email to attendees
**When:** After receiving list  
**Depends on:** Get attendee list from Kyra  
**Notes:** Welcome email to all registered participants

### [ ] Plan workshop content
**When:** Before March 16  
**Notes:** 
- Outline session flow
- Prepare demos/examples
- Create handout (definitions, resources)
- Design pre-event poll questions (if not already done)

### [ ] Pre-event prep
**When:** Day of / day before  
**Notes:**
- Test live setup (streaming, audio, screen share)
- Have recording setup ready
- Review attendee poll responses for tailoring

---

## Key Dates

| Date | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| March 9 | Expect attendee list from Kyra |
| March 13 | Registration closes |
| March 16 | Workshop day |

## Focus: Conservative & Skeptical Approach to AI

A defensive, protective look at AI for homeschooling families:

- Protecting ourselves from AI risks
- Dangers and pitfalls of AI tools
- Current trends and where they're heading
- Responsible use principles
- Defensive mindset for families

## Description

We begin with what matters most: your child's learning and development. We'll consider how AI tools may influence attention, creativity, and study habits, and what responsible use can look like as children grow into teens and prepare for university. Then we'll demystify the current AI landscape—what today's tools can and can't do and where they're heading—including text-based systems (like chatbots), image creation, and video creation/editing.

- A clear view of how AI relates to children's development and learning
- Age-aware guidance for families and older teens approaching university
- A high-level understanding of where AI is today and what's coming next

- A short pre-event poll (sent a few weeks in advance) to tailor the session
- A recording link if you can't attend live

## Notes

This is Part 1 of a two-part series. Part 2 focuses on practical, day-to-day use of AI in homeschooling. We recommend attending this session first.

## Instructor

Gerson Neil Nazareth — 25+ years in software, serves as a CTO. Uses AI extensively in professional software development and is committed to helping families use AI responsibly.
